name: Wasek, Don
evidence: Donald E. Wasek is co-founder (with Arch 'Beaver' Aplin III) of Buc-ee's; filing lists 'Owner, Bucee's'. Travis Central Appraisal District records tie him to a downtown Austin condo (78701 matches) plus a Lake Travis home. Federal FEC record is fully Republican-leaning (lean 0.0, 27 gifts), yet this $5,000 went to Travis Co. Judge Andy Brown (D) — business/access giving.

name: Castillo, Gerardo
resolved_employer: President of ABML (AshBritt Management & Logistics), the government-solutions/disaster-response division of AshBritt
evidence: Gerardo Castillo is President of ABML, AshBritt's government-support/emergency-management & logistics arm (disaster response, USACE wildfire mission); division has generated $1B+ revenue. Holds an MPA from UT-Austin's LBJ School (explains Travis Co. ties). Delray Beach FL address matches AshBritt's SE-FL base. FEC record strongly Democratic (lean 0.99). Note: govt/disaster contractor, not a defense/weapons contractor — no military-defense flag warranted.

name: LaMantia, Samantha
resolved_employer: Owner/GM, Favorite Brands (Texas craft-beer, wine & spirits distributor; LaMantia family company)
evidence: Samantha LaMantia (Lamantia Luchak) is an owner/general manager of Favorite Brands, the LaMantia family's high-end beer/wine/spirits distributorship (Austin, San Antonio, Houston, Dallas, Albuquerque); former Austin Chamber of Commerce board member. The politically active LaMantia family (also L&F Distributors of South Texas) gives across parties; her FEC record is mixed-to-Republican (lean 0.28). Zip 78737 (SW Austin) fits.

name: Tuttle, Nicole
resolved_employer: Homemaker (Not Employed); spouse is G. Tyson Tuttle, former CEO of Silicon Labs / founder-CEO of Circuit
evidence: Nicole Caspers Tuttle co-owns 608 Baylor St, Austin 78703 (zip matches exactly) with husband G. Tyson Tuttle, the former Silicon Laboratories CEO (now founder/CEO of Circuit). Filing lists 'Not Employed'; FEC record fully Democratic (lean 1.0). Couple are known Austin philanthropic/KUT donors. Wealth/tech tie is via spouse; she herself is not employed.

name: Brooks, Lon Hayden
resolved_employer: Real estate broker/investor, Austin (TREC broker since 1988; parks/greenspace advocate)
evidence: Lon Hayden Brooks is a Texas real-estate broker (TREC lic. 295822, issued 1988) at 1804 Lakeshore Dr, Austin 78746 — zip matches exactly. Also an active real-estate investor (properties held via holding companies) and, with an urban-planning/architecture background, serves on ~a dozen public-greenspace advisory boards (Texas Children in Nature). Filing's 'Not Employed'/'None' likely reflects semi-retired status.

name: Lipson, Howard
evidence: Multiple Austin-area Howard Lipsons exist. The most prominent (Howard Lipson, private-equity investor, co-founder of Alignment Artist Capital / Octone Records) is associated with 78746, not the donor's 78757 (Allandale/Crestview). Filing says 'Not Employed'; FEC record fully Democratic (lean 1.0, 20 gifts). Cannot corroborate the specific person by zip or occupation — not classifying.

name: Barnard, Hayes
resolved_employer: Founder, Chairman & CEO, GoodLeap (clean-energy / home-improvement fintech lender; formerly Loanpal)
evidence: Hayes Barnard is founder/chairman/CEO of GoodLeap, the largest U.S. sustainability-focused fintech (financed $20B+ in clean-energy/home loans); ex-SolarCity, founder of GivePower nonprofit. Austin-resident billionaire (Forbes 400). Filing matches 'CEO Founder Chairman, GoodLeap', Austin 78703. FEC record mixed-leaning-Republican (lean 0.27). No fossil-fuel/defense/pro-Israel ties found; GoodLeap is clean-energy financing.

name: McKenzie, Mark
resolved_employer: President/CEO & owner, Ranger Excavating (Central Texas excavation & site-development contractor)
evidence: Best match is Mark McKenzie, president/CEO who bought Ranger Excavating (700+ employees, $400M+ 2022 revenue; site work on COTA F1 track, Tesla Gigafactory, Samsung, Apple campus); B.S. architectural engineering UT-Austin 1986; family runs Austin's Covalent Foundation. Filing has blank occupation/employer, so identity rests on name + wealthy west-Austin 78733 + $5,000 to Travis Co. commissioners Shea & Howard (D). FEC record fully Republican federally (lean 0.0006) despite local Dem giving — access pattern for an infrastructure contractor.

name: Blakeslee, Julie
resolved_employer: Owner/designer, Big Red Sun (Austin landscape-design studio & boutique nursery)
evidence: Julie Blakeslee (formerly Thornton) is the longtime owner/designer of Big Red Sun Austin, a landscape-design studio (clients incl. Jeffrey's, W Hotel, Hotel San Jose); a well-known Austin design/society figure and active Democratic donor (FEC lean 1.0 across 209 gifts — consistent with a prominent local donor). Filing lists 'Not Employed', Austin 78701; identity supported by name + Austin + heavy Dem donor profile, but downtown 78701 residence not independently confirmed. Landscape design mapped to Architecture.

name: Burke, Cecelia
evidence: Cecelia Burke is a former Travis County Tax Assessor-Collector (documented in county archives and a Travis Co. elected-officials endorsement list). Filing shows 'Retired', Austin 78731, and a long, dense record (2017–2025) of small-to-mid gifts spread across Travis Co. Democratic officials — Brown, Travillion, Shea, Howard, Gomez — exactly the pattern of a retired local Democratic public official; FEC lean 0.999 (533 gifts).
